items.claim: Tabitha Richi's striking technique and power are insufficient for professional competition.
items.quote: Her striking sucks. Now she's in great shape, but she doesn't have the technique. She doesn't have the power.

items.claim: Tabitha Richi has abandoned the wrestling-heavy style that previously made her successful.
items.quote: She doesn't shoot takedowns anymore. She doesn't do any of the things that she used to do.

items.claim: Fatima Mle is a superior striker, wrestler, and grappler compared to Tabitha Richi.
items.quote: Fatima will be the better striker, the better wrestler, the better grappler.

items.claim: Chase Hooper lacks athleticism and physicality compared to his peers.
items.quote: What he does lack in athleticism and physicality. He makes up for a high fight IQ and very dangerous BJJ.

items.claim: Chase Hooper is coming off two consecutive knockout losses.
items.quote: He is coming off that second knockout loss in a row.

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez uses leg kicks as a primary setup for his takedowns.
items.quote: He throws a ton of leg kicks. He uses those leg kicks to set up takedowns.

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez lacks the one-punch knockout power possessed by Chase Hooper's previous opponents.
items.quote: he doesn't have anywhere near the power that Chase's last two opponents had.

items.claim: Jared Cannonier is experiencing a decline in physical speed and durability due to age.
items.quote: Jared Canineer, powerful, incredibly dangerous, but also old and starting to slow down.

items.claim: Jared Cannonier has increased his takedown frequency in recent fights to compensate for his declining chin.
items.quote: he does have eight takedowns in his last five fights, and that's because he's old. He's getting older. So, he's trying to adjust his fight style because he's know his chin isn't there anymore.
